title: A Kinder, Gentler Larry Ellison? Oracle Turns New Face to Its User Community
author: Katherine Jones
abstract: Katherine Jones of Aberdeen Group reports on Oracle Apps World 2002 in San Diego, where Oracle shifted its conference message from product launches to persuading its installed base to upgrade to the e-Business Suite 11i. The insight covers Oracle's pivot toward customer satisfaction, its expanded outsourcing/ASP hosting push, the daily financial close feature, and the rapprochement with the Oracle Application User Group. Aberdeen concludes that Oracle's customer-centric turn is positive but that economic headwinds and upgrade complexity will slow adoption.
importance_rationale: Contemporaneous Aberdeen analysis of a pivotal Oracle strategy shift toward customer centricity and SaaS/outsourcing models; documents early signals of the ERP-to-cloud transition well before it became mainstream.
relevance_rationale: The outsourcing-to-cloud trajectory and daily financial close predictions have been fully vindicated; specific Oracle 11i details are dated but the strategic analysis patterns remain instructive.
prescience_rationale: Correctly foresaw the SaaS/outsourcing model for ERP and daily close adoption; Oracle 11i-specific predictions less durable as Oracle moved to cloud applications post-2010.
